# Formation Flight — Model Results

*Auto-generated by `scripts/generate_results.py` on 2026-05-29 19:50.*
*Re-run after model changes to update.*

## Structural Model

Power-law fit: `m_wing = 0.005812 * b^2.406`
- Data range: 25–78 m span
- Exponent 2.406 confirms superlinear scaling

## Reference Comparisons (HALE 20km)

| Config | N | Span(m) | Eff.Span(m) | Mass(kg) | Wing(kg) | Ctrl(kg) | Drag(N) | Power(W) | Cost |

### Key Findings

**4x15m formation vs. single 60m aircraft:**
- Mass savings: 82.8%
- Drag reduction: 84.9%
- Power change: +36.7%

## Position Strategy Impact

4x15m leader/follower, V formation, 10% overlap:

| Strategy | Drag(N) | Power(W) | Cost |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| heavy_front | 3.36 | 761.45 | 121.87 |
| heavy_wake | 3.28 | 759.46 | 121.87 |
| uniform | 3.36 | 761.45 | 121.87 |

## Model Assumptions

- Mission: HALE 20km, altitude 20000m
- Air density: 0.0889 kg/m³, velocity: 25.0 m/s
- Oswald efficiency: 0.85, CD0: 0.025
- Wing loading: 45.0 N/m²
- Formation drag model: Gaussian wake benefit, optimal overlap ~10%
- Leader hardware: ~2.5 kg, 15W; Follower: ~0.4 kg, 3W
- Station-keeping tolerance: 2.0 m

## Caveats

- Wing mass estimates are based on limited public data (all marked 'estimated')
- Formation drag model uses simplified Gaussian wake interaction, not full vortex lattice
- Cost model is mass-proxy only — no dollar values
- Energy balance / battery sizing not yet integrated into sweep results
- No dynamic effects (gusts, control latency, failure modes)
